UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and account information within the HappyChat application. Authentication details are managed by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: A unique, user-chosen handle for identification.

email.description: The user's email address, used for contact and potentially login (via external auth).

fullName.description: The user's full name, displayed in the application.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture or avatar.

bio.description: A short biographical text about the user.

lastActiveAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last activity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Represents a chat conversation, which can be a private chat between two users or a group chat.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

type.description: The type of conversation (e.g., 'private' for 1-to-1 chats, 'group' for multi-user chats).

name.description: The name of the conversation, primarily used for group chats. This is optional for private chats.

adminId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who is the administrator of a group conversation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Conversation)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation was last updated.

participantIds.description: References to UserProfile entities participating in this conversation. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Conversation)

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a single message sent within a conversation, including text, media references, and metadata.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N Message)

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who sent this message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Message)

content.description: The textual content of the message.

sentAt.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

readByParticipantIds.description: References to UserProfile entities who have read this message. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Message)

mediaAttachmentIds.description: References to MediaAttachment entities associated with this message. (Relationship: MediaAttachment 1:N Message, if a message can have multiple attachments).

parentMessageId.description: Optional reference to a parent message if this message is a reply. (Relationship: Message 1:N Message)

MediaAttachment.title: MediaAttachment
MediaAttachment.description: Represents a file or media object shared within a message.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MediaAttachment entity.

messageId.description: Reference to the Message this attachment belongs to. (Relationship: Message 1:N MediaAttachment)

uploaderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded this attachment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MediaAttachment)

filename.description: The original filename of the attached media.

fileType.description: The MIME type of the attached file (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'application/pdf').

fileSizeKB.description: The size of the file in kilobytes.

url.description: The URL where the media file is stored (e.g., in cloud storage).

thumbnailUrl.description: Optional URL to a thumbnail image or video preview.

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the media attachment was uploaded.

MessageReaction.title: MessageReaction
MessageReaction.description: Represents an emoji reaction added by a user to a specific message.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MessageReaction entity.

messageId.description: Reference to the Message this reaction is associated with. (Relationship: Message 1:N MessageReaction)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who added this reaction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MessageReaction)

emoji.description: The Unicode character of the emoji used for the reaction (e.g., "👍", "❤️").

reactedAt.description: Timestamp when the reaction was added.

Call.title: Call
Call.description: Represents a voice or video call event, including its participants and duration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Call entity.

conversationId.description: Optional reference to the Conversation from which this call was initiated. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N Call)

initiatorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the call. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Call)

startTime.description: Timestamp when the call started or was initiated.

endTime.description: Optional timestamp when the call ended.

status.description: The current status of the call (e.g., 'started', 'ended', 'missed', 'declined', 'ringing').

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the call in seconds, if completed.

participantIds.description: References to UserProfile entities who participated in or were invited to the call. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Call)

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) matches the Firebase Authentication `uid`. This collection uses path-based ownership; `request.auth.uid` must match `userId` for full write access. Specific public fields may be readable by other authenticated users.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user profile, corresponding to the user's Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores chat conversations (private or group). Includes a denormalized 'participantIds' array and 'adminId' for group chats, which are critical for authorization independence and QAPs. Users can only access conversations if their `uid` is in `participantIds`.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the conversation.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a conversation. Each message document includes denormalized 'conversationParticipantIds' and 'conversationAdminId' (copied from the parent conversation) to ensure authorization independence without requiring `get()` calls to the parent document.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

definition.description: Stores media attachments for a specific message. Each attachment document includes denormalized 'conversationParticipantIds' and 'conversationAdminId' (copied from the parent conversation) for authorization independence.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent message.

params.name: attachmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the media attachment.

definition.description: Stores emoji reactions to a specific message. Each reaction document includes denormalized 'conversationParticipantIds' and 'conversationAdminId' (copied from the parent conversation) for authorization independence.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent message.

params.name: reactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message reaction.

definition.description: Stores information about voice or video calls. Authorization is handled by the 'participantIds' array within the Call document itself, ensuring independence and supporting QAPs for listing a user's calls.

params.name: callId
params.description: The unique identifier for the call.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for HappyChat prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Accurate Projections (QAPs) by heavily utilizing denormalization and structural segregation. Each collection is designed to support direct, non-hierarchical authorization checks using only the `request.auth.uid` and the document's own fields, thus eliminating expensive and brittle `get()` calls within security rules.

1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored in a top-level collection. Each document's ID (`userId`) is directly tied to the Firebase Authentication `uid` of the owner. This ensures path-based ownership, making rules for creating, reading, updating, and deleting profiles straightforward (`request.auth.uid == userId`). For QAPs, users can list their own profile by path. For other users, specific fields like `username`, `profileImageUrl`, and `status` can be made publicly readable to authenticated users for displaying participant lists or user lookups without exposing sensitive data, while full profile writes are restricted to the owner.

2.  **Conversations (`/conversations/{conversationId}`):** Conversations are stored in a top-level collection. Authorization relies on the `participantIds` array (a list of all user UIDs involved in the conversation) directly present within each `Conversation` document. This denormalized field is critical for both authorization independence and QAPs. A user can securely list all their conversations by performing a query like `db.collection('conversations').where('participantIds', 'array-contains', request.auth.uid)`. Access rules for read/write on a conversation document will simply check if `request.auth.uid` is present in `resource.data.participantIds`.

3.  **Messages, Media Attachments, and Reactions (Nested under Conversations):** To maintain Authorization Independence for subcollections like Messages (`/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}`), Media Attachments (`/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}/mediaAttachments/{attachmentId}`), and Message Reactions (`/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}/reactions/{reactionId}`), the crucial `participantIds` and `adminId` from the parent `Conversation` MUST be denormalized and copied into every document within these subcollections. For instance, each `Message` document will include `conversationParticipantIds` and `conversationAdminId`. This allows security rules for these subcollections to evaluate access rights (e.g., `request.auth.uid in resource.data.conversationParticipantIds`) without needing to perform a `get()` call to the parent `Conversation` document. This directly supports QAPs as a user can query for messages within a specific conversation (`/conversations/{conversationId}/messages`) and the rules will efficiently filter based on the message's own denormalized fields, assuming the `conversationId` itself is known to be accessible by the user.

4.  **Calls (`/calls/{callId}`):** Calls are stored in a top-level collection. Similar to conversations, access control is managed by the `participantIds` array within the `Call` document itself. This allows for independent authorization checks and enables QAPs for listing a user's calls (`db.collection('calls').where('participantIds', 'array-contains', request.auth.uid)`).

This design ensures that all authorization decisions are atomic and self-contained within the document being accessed or its immediate request data, thereby fulfilling the CRITICAL mandate of Authorization Independence and facilitating robust, debuggable security rules and efficient queries.
